parameterGroups.label: Output
parameterGroups.rationale: Base-class output controls shared by all SoundGenerators

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: The output volume as normalised linear gain (0.0 to 1.0), not decibels. Use a SimpleGain effect in the FX chain for decibel-scaled volume control.

parameterGroups.label: Voice Management
parameterGroups.rationale: Controls how voices are allocated and killed

parameters.name: Kill Fade Time
parameters.description: The fade out time in milliseconds when voices are killed by exceeding the voice limit or by a voice killer

parameters.name: Kill Second Voices
parameters.description: Kills retriggered voices instead of allowing multiple notes

parameterGroups.label: FM Synthesis
parameterGroups.rationale: Controls for FM synthesis between two child synths - EnableFM gates the CarrierIndex and ModulatorIndex parameters

parameters.name: Enable FM
parameters.description: Enables FM synthesis between two child synths

parameters.name: Carrier Index
parameters.description: The child synth index used as FM carrier

parameters.name: Modulator Index
parameters.description: The child synth index used as FM modulator

parameterGroups.label: Unison
parameterGroups.rationale: Unison voice stacking with detune and stereo spread - UnisonoVoiceAmount controls count, UnisonoDetune and UnisonoSpread have dedicated modulation chains

parameters.name: Unison Voice Amount
parameters.description: The number of unison voices per note

parameters.name: Unison Detune
parameters.description: The detune spread in semitones for unison voices

parameters.name: Unison Spread
parameters.description: The stereo spread of unison voices (0 = mono, 1 = full width)

parameterGroups.label: Output Mode
parameterGroups.rationale: Global output mode toggle that forces monophonic summing

parameters.name: Force Mono
parameters.description: Forces monophonic output by summing all voices

standaloneModChains.name: Pitch Modulation
standaloneModChains.note: Modulates implicit base pitch of all child synths. Shared across children - this is the key feature that distinguishes SynthGroup from SynthChain.

gaps.question: How does SynthGroup share modulation across child synths? Does it override the children's own modulation chains, or does it apply additional modulation on top? What exactly does 'shared modulation' mean in the rendering pipeline?
gaps.context: This is the core feature that distinguishes SynthGroup from SynthChain - need precise understanding for the reference page.

gaps.question: How is FM synthesis implemented? When EnableFM is on, how are the carrier and modulator child synths connected? Is the modulator's audio output used to modulate the carrier's frequency? What happens to other (non-carrier, non-modulator) children?
gaps.context: FM synthesis is a major feature - need to show the signal flow accurately.

gaps.question: How are unison voices rendered? Does each unison voice get a separate pitch offset and pan position? Is the detune spread distributed symmetrically (e.g. -6, -3, +3, +6 semitones for 4 voices at 12st detune)?
gaps.context: Need to explain the unison rendering model clearly in the pseudo-code.

gaps.question: Does UnisonoVoiceAmount multiply the actual voice count? E.g. 8 unison voices with 16 note polyphony = 128 voice renders?
gaps.context: Critical performance information for users - unison can dramatically increase CPU usage.

gaps.question: The constrainer '!ModulatorSynthChain|!GlobalModulatorContainer|!ModulatorSynthGroup|!MacroModulationSource' excludes these types. Why are these specific types excluded? Is it because they can't participate in shared modulation or FM?
gaps.context: Users need to know what they can and cannot add as children.

gaps.question: The base data has both fx_constrainer '*' and child_fx_constrainer 'VoiceEffect'. What does child_fx_constrainer mean - does it restrict what effect types can be added to child synths' FX chains when they are inside this group?
gaps.context: Need to document this constraint clearly since it affects how users build patches inside a SynthGroup.

gaps.question: Where in the signal path does ForceMono apply? After unison spread, before FX chain? Does it sum L+R to mono?
gaps.context: Need to place this correctly in the pseudo-code.

gaps.question: What exactly does KillSecondVoices do? The description says 'kills retriggered voices' - does this mean it enforces legato/mono behaviour, or something specific to unison voice management?
gaps.context: The default is On (1.0) which is unusual - need to understand the specific behaviour.

gaps.question: CarrierIndex and ModulatorIndex default to -1. What does -1 mean - auto-detect first/second child, or FM disabled?
gaps.context: Users need to know what the default values mean and how to configure FM.

gaps.question: What is the full rendering order? Specifically: shared modulation -> child rendering -> unison processing -> FM -> gain -> FX chain? Or a different order?
gaps.context: Need to author accurate pseudo-code showing the complete signal flow.

gaps.question: Does SynthGroup have a custom editor or FloatingTile content type?

notes: SynthGroup is the advanced container in HISE, distinct from SynthChain in three ways: (1) it shares modulation chains (gain, pitch) across all child synths rather than each child having independent modulation; (2) it supports FM synthesis between two designated child synths; (3) it supports unison voice stacking with per-voice detune and stereo spread. The constrainer excludes SynthChain, SynthGroup, GlobalModulatorContainer, and MacroModulationSource as children - these container-level modules cannot participate in shared modulation. The child_fx_constrainer 'VoiceEffect' suggests that child synths inside a SynthGroup can only use polyphonic effects (not master effects). The Gain Modulation chain allows all modulator types (constrainer '*') unlike SynthChain which restricts to TimeVariantModulator only. The Pitch Modulation chain is enabled (unlike SynthChain where it is disabled) because shared pitch modulation is a core feature.
